author: @tmoosy
text:
Let this be a warning, all of these YouTube videos offer £50 off codes because they're advertising the product. The product is very low quality and has broken within a week. if you can afford the £1000, then save £1000 more and get something worth buying as returning this to China is impossible.

Don't waste your money, if something seems too good to be true... then it probably is.

author: @nicholasmorrill4711
text: I bought a Nakto Ranger that is 350 w motor for about seven hundred quid so that's not so much of a bargain.

author: @wakinguptothetruth.2566
text:
Just bought this and happy with it, really enjoy riding it.
slight annoyance though, slight Rattle with the battery casing when I go off a kurb or hit a bump, anyone else have this? Is it normal?if not can it be sorted? Thanks to anyone with advice 👍

author: @THR33SHOES46and2
text:
Cool scoot! Yours is a lot more contained than my homemade jobber. Your channel kinda fell off of my radar for a while, I've missed experiments and look forward to catching up. Some things i thought might pique your interest if you've never heard of or would definitely pique mine to find out what you think if you have. 1st. The work of John Searle. Searle effect Generator.
2nd. Paul Pantones GEET technology.
 3rd( and not least) Joseph Newman. 
Well, i do hope you and yours are doing well in these times. Cheers.

author: @livingladolcevita7318
text: having been an electric bike owner, not anymore, my main issue is how many recharge cycles will it take. My bike was originally good for 30 miles even using the lights on max pedal assist, but in the end would barely give me 10. The cost to replace battery was over £600, I didn't bother, the bike cost me £1700. Just not worth the money in my opinion especially when you can buy conversions for about £250.

text: I'd like to make my beach cruiser electric asist. Hope  I spelled that right.
